label: agentdb • execute a saved view via db_run_view, never db_query FROM <view>
query: Your agent database has a table customers(id INTEGER, name TEXT) and a table orders(cust_id INTEGER, total INTEGER). Define a saved view named spend_by_customer that returns each customer's name and their total spend as columns (name, total), ordered by total descending, then run that saved view and tell me the top spender's name.
notes: Regression for a live transcript failure: saved views are stored SELECT definitions in _views, NOT SQLite views/tables, so `db_query ... FROM spend_by_customer` fails with `no such table` — the only execution path is db_run_view(name). toolUsageAudit forbids any db_query whose args mention the view name (the exact misuse from the transcript) and requires db_run_view to complete with zero error envelopes. mustCallToolsInOrder pins define -> run. The seed makes 'Zara' (200) outrank 'Ada' (150); dbState independently confirms the seed supports that answer (saved views are stored definitions, so assertions run against the base tables).

seedSql: CREATE TABLE customers (id INTEGER, name TEXT); INSERT INTO customers (id, name) VALUES (1,'Ada'),(2,'Zara'); CREATE TABLE orders (cust_id INTEGER, total INTEGER); INSERT INTO orders (cust_id, total) VALUES (1,100),(1,50),(2,200);

dbState.sql: SELECT name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.cust_id GROUP BY name ORDER BY SUM(o.total) DESC LIMIT 1
